synopsis: The Session of Trinity Presbyterian Church, Slidell, Louisiana complained that Grace Presbytery violated proper procedure when it delayed the ordination of Adrian Pater, a licentiate who had been examined and sustained for ordination as an evangelist. The Judicial Commission found that inadequate minutes were presented and returned the matter to Grace Presbytery for a new hearing, while recommending that the General Assembly affirm that the BCO does not envision ordination expressly to pastoral services in churches of other denominations.
notes: The case decision appears at pages 52-72 in the minutes. The complaint was filed on pages 50-51 (printed pages 50-51 of the GA minutes). The Judicial Commission's report was presented on page 72 (4-65). The Assembly voted 238-131 to adopt the Commission's recommendation that the BCO does not envision ordination to pastoral services in churches of other denominations. A motion for reconsideration was made (4-72) but no changes were made to the previous action.
